mnemonics: ARM Instructions Are Primitive Operations (ARM = Arithmetic, Register, Memory instructions are the core primitive operations)
Every Programmer Must Initialize Registers (EPMIR - reminds you that registers must be properly set before use)
Data Processing Instructions Alter Flags (DPIAF - reminds you these instructions affect condition codes)
keyPoints: Machine instructions are the primitive operations a CPU can execute directly.
Instructions operate on data stored in registers or memory locations.
Programs are sequences of instructions that manipulate data and control program flow.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that the destination register is typically the first operand in ARM assembly (e.g., MOV R0, R1 moves R1 to R0).
Confusing the roles of source and destination operands in instructions, leading to incorrect data flow.
Misunderstanding how condition flags are set by instructions, leading to incorrect branch behavior.
